examples: The proceedings of Parliament are published each day by Hansard.
Dissatisfaction with how MPs are doing their job rose by eight points, to 44%, over the past year, according to a survey by Ipsos MORI for the Hansard Society.
Since 1980 the courts have been permitted to look at Hansard (the official report of the debates and proceedings in the House of Commons) as an external aid to the construction of a statute.

Three quarters of people are unable to name their MP, according to an annual Hansard Society survey.
